student_verification.name: student
student_verification.declaration: I used Claude to help identify and phrase the three assumptions. I verified each assumption against the paper myself and confirmed the section/table references are accurate. The violation scenarios are grounded in real Twitter language behaviour that I understand.

interactions.response_summary: Generated three assumptions with the required format: assumption, why needed, violation scenario, paper reference. Assumptions covered emoticon reliability, context-independent prior polarity, and newswire POS tagger transferability.

interactions.prompt: why do we need this assumption: Newswire POS tagger works on tweets and like what would be different if we don't keep this assumption
interactions.response_summary: Explained why the POS tagger assumption matters and what happens when it breaks — corrupts f1, f5, f8 features which are the most important group.

interactions.prompt: ohk understood but can you also help me write these things shorter in bullets so as to be more readable and also check on the instructions if this follow that
interactions.response_summary: Rewrote all three assumptions into tight 2-3 bullet format per field, trimmed to match rubric's 2-3 sentence requirement, removed the unnecessary note cell.
